title: Alpine Cheese-Making Traditions
extract: High in the Swiss Alps, the tradition of making cheese during summer months goes back centuries. Every June, farmers still drive their cattle up to high pastures in a celebration called Alpaufzug, decorating the cows with flowers and enormous bells. The milk from cows grazing on alpine wildflowers produces a distinctively flavored cheese that cannot be replicated in lowland dairies. My grandfather spent every summer in a wooden hut at 2,000 meters, stirring massive copper vats over wood fires. The knowledge passes from generation to generation through practice, not textbooks—you learn when the curd is ready by how it feels between your fingers.
